source: latency: eval/reports/report-rtx-3060.md (7 original models); memory: measured on the RTX 3090 (outputs/ci/report.md). New models (pi05, vla_adapter, openvla_oft): latency + memory measured on this RTX 3090 (model cards + libero_object task-0 smoke).
note: Server-side latency = mean `total` ms per vla-server call; memory = peak VRAM (nvidia-smi --query-compute-apps). peak_vram_mib is the ACTUAL RTX 3090 measurement (a 3090 reserves larger CUDA buffers than the 3060, so it is rebaselined here). For the 7 original models server_total_ms is still the prior RTX 3060 sweep as a 'no slower than the 3060' bar (the 3090 meets-or-beats it). For the new models (pi05, vla_adapter, openvla_oft) there is no 3060 sweep, so server_total_ms is the RTX 3090 measurement itself (pi05 from its smoke server log; vla_adapter/openvla_oft from their model cards) and the 1.10x tolerance is the headroom; client_call_ms for these is an estimate (server_total_ms + ~23 ms orchestrator overhead) and is not gated; sr_reported is a single-task (task-0, 10-ep) smoke, not a full sweep. Gate = actual <= tolerance * baseline.
